target_buyer: Non-technical founders, citizen developers, and indie hackers leveraging no-code platforms to quickly build and launch AI-powered applications; high willingness to pay.
reason: The synergy between no-code platforms and AI is exploding, empowering non-technical users to launch sophisticated AI applications with pre-built templates.

target_buyer: Developers and technical indie hackers rapidly prototyping new AI-infused SaaS applications with modern web frameworks; very high willingness to pay.
reason: The demand for full-stack boilerplate code for AI-powered SaaS solutions built with popular frameworks like Next.js is skyrocketing.

target_buyer: Creators, influencers, freelancers, and solopreneurs optimizing their content workflows, project management, and revenue tracking in Notion; high willingness to pay.
reason: Creators are entering a Q3 planning cycle, seeking robust systems to streamline operations and maximize output with Notion's evolving features.

target_buyer: Notion power-users and productivity enthusiasts desiring enhanced personal knowledge management systems leveraging AI for synthesis, organization, and retrieval; high willingness to pay.
reason: The integration of AI capabilities into Notion drives demand for next-generation 'Second Brain' templates that leverage these new features.

target_buyer: AI power-users, developers, and AI architects exploring and building complex collaborative AI agent systems; very high willingness to pay.
format: ZIP | Notion template | Diagrams | Code snippets
reason: The emergence of sophisticated collaborative AI agent architectures requires structured design and deployment tools to manage complexity.

target_buyer: AI power-users, developers building agentic systems, keen to implement new frameworks; high willingness to pay.
reason: New open-source AI agent frameworks are gaining production readiness, driving demand for practical implementation guides and pre-configured setups.

target_buyer: Indie hackers, non-technical founders, and marketers adapting to new marketing and growth realities with advanced AI tools and automation; high willingness to pay.
reason: As AI tools evolve, growth strategies must adapt, creating demand for playbooks that outline new, AI-driven growth hacking tactics.

target_buyer: Developers, data scientists, AI power-users looking to create specialized, domain-specific AI models; very high willingness to pay.
reason: Increased demand for specialized AI applications is driving the need for practical, easy-to-follow guides on fine-tuning large language models.

target_buyer: Customer support managers, small business owners, and creators optimizing customer interactions with AI chatbots and support agents; medium willingness to pay.
reason: Businesses are increasingly deploying AI in customer support, creating demand for refined prompt templates to ensure efficient and high-quality interactions.

target_buyer: Creators, marketers, sales professionals looking to leverage new specialized AI models for content, outreach, and lead generation; medium willingness to pay.
reason: Verticalized AI models for sales and marketing are becoming more sophisticated, requiring specialized prompt engineering for optimal results.

target_buyer: Founders, consultants, academics, and knowledge workers automating research tasks with advanced AI models; high willingness to pay.
reason: Enhanced capabilities of AI models are making sophisticated, personalized research automation accessible, driving demand for setup guides.

target_buyer: Developers and technical founders integrating a wave of new AI APIs and services into existing software stacks and workflows; high willingness to pay.
reason: The continuous launch of new specialized AI APIs requires developers to quickly learn and integrate them, creating demand for practical guides.

target_buyer: Creators, marketers, content agencies leveraging AI writing tools and needing ethical and effective strategies to avoid content fatigue; medium willingness to pay.
reason: Creators and businesses are seeking frameworks to effectively integrate AI into their content strategy without compromising quality or authenticity.

target_buyer: Data analysts, non-technical founders, and Notion/Airtable users streamlining data preparation for AI tools and analytics; medium willingness to pay.
format: Notion template | Airtable base | Zapier configs
reason: The increasing reliance on clean data for AI models and analytics drives demand for automated, no-code solutions for data cleaning.

target_buyer: Indie hackers, non-technical founders, solopreneurs needing structured Q2 close-out and Q3 strategic planning tools; medium willingness to pay.
reason: Seasonal demand for businesses and solopreneurs conducting mid-year performance reviews and setting goals for the second half of the year.

target_buyer: Freelance developers, designers, and agencies looking to scale their income by turning services into repeatable, high-margin products; high willingness to pay.
reason: The solopreneur economy continues to grow, with professionals seeking methods to productize their skills for scalable, recurring revenue.

target_buyer: Indie hackers, non-technical founders, and early-stage startups planning late Q2/early Q3 product launches; medium willingness to pay.
reason: A recurring wave of indie hackers and startups prepare for Product Hunt launches, seeking updated, battle-tested strategies.

target_buyer: Legal professionals, compliance officers, and founders in highly regulated industries using AI for document analysis and review; very high willingness to pay.
reason: Increased regulatory scrutiny and the need for efficiency are driving adoption of AI in legal and compliance, creating demand for specialized prompt engineering.

target_buyer: Solopreneurs and indie hackers refining their financial projections, revenue models, and business plans for greater stability and growth; medium willingness to pay.
reason: Solopreneurs require robust financial planning tools as they conduct mid-year reviews and adjust their business models for future profitability.

target_buyer: Marketers, small business owners, and creators planning strategic marketing initiatives and campaigns for the Q3 sales cycle; medium willingness to pay.
reason: Businesses and creators are gearing up for Q3, necessitating structured planners to organize and execute their marketing campaigns effectively.

target_buyer: AI developers, project managers, compliance teams, and consultants focused on responsible AI development and deployment; very high willingness to pay.
reason: As AI adoption scales, ethical considerations, fairness, and bias detection become critical, driving demand for structured audit tools.

target_buyer: Productivity enthusiasts, solopreneurs, and small teams realigning objectives and key results for the latter half of the year; low to medium willingness to pay.
reason: Individuals and teams perform mid-year reviews, driving demand for tools to adjust and track Objectives and Key Results for renewed focus.

target_buyer: Developers, front-end engineers, and indie hackers within niche aesthetic coding communities; medium willingness to pay.
reason: Growing niche developer communities, often centered around specific aesthetics ('vibe coding'), are seeking specialized UI components and design systems.

target_buyer: Indie hackers, solopreneurs, and small business owners reviewing mid-year financial performance and adjusting forecasts; low to medium willingness to pay.
reason: Founders are reviewing mid-year financial performance and adjusting their budgets and forecasts for the remainder of the year.

target_buyer: Developers, open-source contributors, and technical founders seeking to improve project documentation and presentation; low willingness to pay.
reason: Growing emphasis on clear, professional documentation for open-source projects and portfolios is driving demand for high-quality README templates.
